objective: Build a dynamic profit-driven research desk: improve ETF/A-share/options radar quality, data freshness, action-card UX, paper-trade review, backtesting discipline, and risk-aware decision support toward the monthly sprint target.

sources.name: AKShare
sources.why_watch: China-market data connector ideas and data-source coverage.

sources.name: vn.py
sources.why_watch: China-focused quant trading architecture, gateways, CTA, portfolio, and option strategy modules.

sources.name: Qlib
sources.why_watch: Research pipeline, factor modeling, dataset handling, and model evaluation patterns.

sources.name: vectorbt
sources.why_watch: Fast vectorized backtesting and parameter research patterns.

sources.name: backtesting.py
sources.why_watch: Small and readable backtesting API that fits this project's current scale.

sources.name: PyBroker
sources.why_watch: Active Python backtesting and research framework with machine-learning workflows, walkforward evaluation, and broker separation that fit research-first experimentation.

sources.name: Backtrader
sources.why_watch: Readable event-driven backtesting patterns for turning action-card rules into T+1, stop, take-profit, and sizing experiments.

sources.name: learn_backtrader
sources.why_watch: Tutorial-style Backtrader examples for quickly converting simple rules into reproducible backtest notebooks.

sources.name: RQAlpha
sources.why_watch: A-share-oriented backtesting API design and risk/account simulation ideas; research reference only.

sources.name: QUANTAXIS
sources.why_watch: Chinese market data, account, and workflow architecture reference; evaluate freshness before copying patterns.

sources.name: QuantStats
sources.why_watch: Performance tear sheets, drawdown, and return-analysis report ideas.

sources.name: PyPortfolioOpt
sources.why_watch: Portfolio construction reference covering efficient frontier, Black-Litterman, and hierarchical risk parity for ETF basket sizing and risk budgeting.

sources.name: Riskfolio-Lib
sources.why_watch: Broader portfolio-optimization and risk-measure library for stress-testing allocation ideas before they influence action cards.

sources.name: skfolio
sources.why_watch: Scikit-learn style portfolio optimization and cross-validation toolkit useful for robust ETF allocation, model selection, and stress-test workflows.

sources.name: OpenBB
sources.why_watch: Financial data platform and agent/data-access UX patterns.

sources.name: Anthropic financial-services
sources.why_watch: Agent + skill + connector reference design for finance workflows; adapt the human sign-off and staged output pattern.

sources.name: Scrapling
sources.why_watch: Modern public-web scraping framework reference for documentation/article source intake; use only for permitted public pages, not for bypassing access controls.

sources.name: OpenBB Agents
sources.why_watch: Study how finance data access, command UX, and research workflows are organized for a dynamic terminal-like experience.

sources.name: QuantConnect Lean
sources.why_watch: Mature research-to-backtest-to-live engine architecture. Use as architecture reference only.

sources.name: FinRL
sources.why_watch: Reinforcement-learning trading environments and evaluation ideas. Use cautiously.

sources.name: Qbot
sources.why_watch: AI quant research platform reference for end-to-end research, backtest, and visualization patterns; use as study reference, not automatic execution.

sources.name: FinGPT
sources.why_watch: Financial LLM, sentiment, and RAG workflow reference for research summaries and evidence extraction; never use model output as a direct buy signal.

sources.name: ai_quant_trade
sources.why_watch: Chinese AI quant learning map spanning indicators, factors, ML/RL, and examples; use as study syllabus and sample-source index, not production trading code.

sources.name: MilleXi stock_trading
sources.why_watch: LSTM plus reinforcement-learning stock-trading example with Gradio UI; useful for education and experiment design, not as a trading signal engine.

sources.name: czsc
sources.why_watch: Chan theory technical-analysis toolkit for fractals, strokes, multi-level signals, and backtest examples; validate every signal before use.

sources.name: SmartStock-AI-Kit
sources.why_watch: Hardware-plus-AI watch terminal UX reference for voice prompts, alert surfaces, and compact market displays; observe only.

sources.name: CTBZStock
sources.why_watch: A-share simulated/live trading project to study environment separation, Feishu notifications, and broker/API boundary risks; do not copy auto-trading behavior.

sources.name: easytrader
sources.why_watch: A-share client/miniqmt automation reference for broker-boundary research only; keep behind paper-account and human-confirmation gates.

sources.name: ths_trade
sources.why_watch: Old 同花顺 automation API example useful for queueing, logging, and failure-mode review; do not connect to unattended live orders.

sources.name: THSTrader
sources.why_watch: Mobile 同花顺 simulation automation reference for UI-state and focus-risk analysis; research/paper mode only.

sources.name: AI Trading Journal
sources.why_watch: Trading journal and AI-assistant UI reference for improving paper-trade review, conversation capture, and post-trade attribution.

sources.name: TA-Lib
sources.why_watch: Technical-indicator implementation reference for standardizing MACD, RSI, Bollinger, ATR, and volatility features.

sources.name: TuShare
sources.why_watch: A-share data and fundamentals connector candidate; keep credentials in environment and compare against iFind/Eastmoney coverage.

sources.name: efinance
sources.why_watch: Widely used China-market Python data library covering stocks, funds, and futures; useful as a public cross-check layer for ETF and A-share monitoring when comparing Eastmoney-style fields.

sources.name: mootdx
sources.why_watch: Maintained Tongdaxin-compatible A-share data connector for public-market redundancy and field-validation when iFind or web sources disagree.

sources.name: yfinance
sources.why_watch: US ETF and global-market fallback data connector for QDII context and overseas AI/semiconductor watchlists.

sources.name: QuantLib
sources.why_watch: Options, rates, and risk-modeling reference for future option-simulation improvements.

sources.name: OCC Options Disclosure Document
sources.why_watch: Official options-risk baseline and terminology reference for keeping option simulations and review notes grounded in exchange-traded product mechanics.

sources.name: Alpaca Paper Trading Docs
sources.why_watch: Official paper-trading reference that clearly separates simulation from live execution and documents fill, slippage, and queue-position limits.

sources.name: IBKR Paper Trading Accounts
sources.why_watch: Official paper-trading limitations reference with details on simulated fills, unsupported order types, and market-data linkage that help keep review notes honest about simulator drift.

sources.name: Cboe Options Institute Learning Portal
sources.why_watch: Official listed-options education portal with structured derivatives courses, Greeks, and volatility learning paths to ground options research before adding complexity.

sources.name: CCXT
sources.why_watch: Unified crypto exchange API for market-data and connector-design reference; crypto-only and not a direct A-share execution dependency.

sources.name: Freqtrade
sources.why_watch: Crypto bot architecture reference for configuration, backtesting, and risk controls. Use only as design reference, not as A-share execution logic.

sources.name: Hummingbot
sources.why_watch: Market-making and connector architecture reference for risk controls and exchange abstraction; crypto-only, not an A-share priority.

sources.name: BingoCrypto Binance Futures Dashboard
sources.why_watch: Crypto monitoring dashboard UX reference for filters, alerts, table sorting, and rule highlighting; do not copy futures signals.

sources.name: TradeMatcher match-engine
sources.why_watch: Java matching-engine architecture reference for order types, event sourcing, latency claims, and risk/accounting separation; architecture study only.

sources.name: lightning-engine
sources.why_watch: Go in-memory matching-engine reference for orderbook structure, gRPC boundary, and downstream settlement separation; architecture study only.

sources.name: awesome-quant
sources.why_watch: Curated quant finance libraries and learning resources.

sources.name: best-of-algorithmic-trading
sources.why_watch: Ranked ecosystem map for algo-trading frameworks, APIs, and communities.

sources.name: r/algotrading
sources.why_watch: Community discussion about practical strategy testing, execution pitfalls, and tooling.

sources.name: r/quantfinance
sources.why_watch: Quant career, research, modeling, and project discussions.

sources.name: Trading Strategy Communities
sources.why_watch: Curated list of algorithmic trading communities and forums.

sources.name: Dashboard UX Design Principles
sources.why_watch: Keep dashboard improvements focused on actions, thresholds, hierarchy, and avoiding KPI overload.

sources.name: Finance KPI Dashboard Best Practices
sources.why_watch: Use as a UX checklist: top metrics first, actionable context, audience-specific hierarchy.

sources.name: vn.py community
sources.why_watch: Chinese quant trading community and docs around event-driven trading systems.

sources.name: Shanghai Stock Exchange ETF Hub
sources.why_watch: Official SSE ETF portal linking fund information, market data, training, and rule resources for Shanghai-listed ETF monitoring and reference checks.

sources.name: Shenzhen Stock Exchange Fund Market Data
sources.why_watch: Official SZSE fund market-data entry point with fund lists, ETF lists, disclosures, and rules useful for Shenzhen ETF coverage validation.

sources.name: AI Quant Research Director SOP
sources.why_watch: Local methodology card: use AI as implementation analyst, while user defines hypothesis, risk gates, and acceptance criteria.

sources.name: Factor Research Playbook
sources.why_watch: Local factor-validation template for converting trading intuition into computable factors, event studies, and action-card gates.

sources.name: Power Equipment Supercycle Theme Card
sources.why_watch: Local theme card for AI data-center power equipment, gas turbine components, and composite insulators; use only as watchlist evidence.

sources.name: Quant Methodology Source Triage 2026-06-04
sources.why_watch: Local triage table for user-pasted quant methodology sources; separates immediate action-card upgrades from long-term research.

sources.name: Self-Evolving Skill Stock Selection Experiment List
sources.why_watch: Local experiment checklist for turning Huatai self-evolving Skill ideas into hypothesis, factor, backtest, audit, and action-card loops.

sources.name: Level2 Informed Trading Proxy Plan
sources.why_watch: Local proxy plan for Level2 informed-trading factors using iFind volume, amount-ratio, MA-distance, and theme resonance until Level2 is available.
